conbody.p: Ghosts are a central symbol of Halloween, commonly representing spirits of the dead and the lingering presence of the past. Their association with haunted locations, unfinished business, and the supernatural has made them a recurring figure in folklore and popular culture.

section.title: Folklore and Cultural Traditions
section.p: Stories of ghosts appear in the traditions of many cultures, often describing spirits that remain in the human world due to unresolved emotions or obligations. These narratives frequently associate ghosts with specific places, such as homes, battlefields, or burial grounds.
Over time, such stories influenced seasonal observances connected to remembrance of the dead, helping establish ghosts as a symbolic presence during Halloween.

section.title: Symbolic Meaning
section.p: Symbolically, ghosts often represent memory, loss, and the boundary between life and death. They may also embody fear of the unknown or anxiety about what lies beyond the visible world.
In Halloween contexts, ghost imagery can range from frightening to playful, allowing the symbol to be used for both suspense and humor.

section.title: Modern Halloween Usage
section.p: In modern Halloween celebrations, ghosts are depicted in a wide variety of forms, from realistic apparitions to simplified, cartoon-like figures. They are commonly used in decorations, costumes, and themed entertainment.
Ghost imagery is especially popular in children’s decorations due to its flexibility as a symbol that can be adapted to different levels of intensity.

example.title: Common Uses of Ghosts During Halloween

ul.li: Costumes depicting friendly or frightening ghosts
Haunted house themes and storytelling activities
